metadata.title:
What CITY COUNCIL HAS DONE IN


TWO MONTHS POLITICAL F|DDL|NC

THAT WAGE ORDINANCE

pages.raw_text:
DECEMBER 1, the city council
directed the corporation counsel to
draft an ordinance granting an in
crease of rs cents per day to COM
mon laborers and SO cents per day
10 teamsters.


DECEMBER Is, rejected the
wage petition and countermanded
The order of December 1.


DECEMBER 81, jumped the fence
again and gave second order to cor
poration counsel to draft an ordi-
nance increasing the wages.


JANUARY 10, evidently under
the influence of boss men with
money, killed the second order OF
December 81.


JANUARY IA, with plenty of evil
dence of cold feet" the council
threw the whole troublesome mat
ter into the hands Of the mayor for
his Recommendation"


FEBRUARY 20, on recommends
ton of Mayor Pratt and faced by a
counter petition by many influen-
tial business men, council again
adopted resolutions favoring wage
scale and for the third time direct
ed that an ordinance granting same
be drafted.


FEBRUARY "a, for some reason
not altogether clear, council leaped
back across the moneyed side Of
the political fence and for the third
time killed the order for the pro-
posed increased wage scale ordi-
nance.
